analysis.reasoning: The tweet foregrounds anti-corruption reforms, the establishment of a new commission under international and national law, rejection of donor influence, a claim that the EU has not blocked investigations, and a reaffirmation of the primacy of functioning national institutions. These elements align with narratives that portray the EU as a global advocate for fair governance (EU5), a contributor to a better world through governance and rule of law (EU6), and policies/actions that are useful for citizens (EU3).
